scenario_description: The notice contains multiple dates. The user says to update the date but doesn't specify which one. The agent asks, then only changes the publication date.

command:
printf 'System Maintenance Notice

Publication Date: March 15, 2025

Dear Colleagues,

The IT Department will perform routine system maintenance soon. Details are as follows:

Maintenance Window: April 2, 2025, 10:00 PM to 2:00 AM
Affected Systems: ERP, OA, Email

Important Reminders:
1. Please save all critical data before April 2, 2025, 8:00 PM.
2. For urgent matters during maintenance, contact IT on-call: 021-99887766.
3. Systems are expected to be restored by April 3, 2025, 2:30 AM.

IT Operations Department  March 15, 2025
' > /home/user/Desktop/announcement.txt

DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Help me update the date in the file
Only update the Publication Date on the first line to "July 1, 2025", leave all other dates unchanged, then save
